title: pseudodanthonia trigyna
intro: Pseudodanthonia trigyna, also known as the Australian sawgrass, is a type of grass found in eastern Australia, particularly in New South Wales and Queensland. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Pseudodanthonia trigyna include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
